question_text: A motor has an efficiency of 80%. If 500 J of electrical energy is supplied to the motor, how much energy is wasted as heat?
explanation: Efficiency tells us what fraction of input energy is usefully converted. If efficiency is 80%, then 20% of energy is wasted. To find wasted energy: 20% of 500 J = 0.20 × 500 = 100 J. The remaining 400 J is converted to useful work by the motor.
